behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git history + branch age + PR absence across module boundaries. A concrete miss below System: with mocked git outputs, detection can pass while real `git merge-base`/`rev-list` semantics mark the branch differently (for example due to ancestry nuances), so stale branches are misclassified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, create `main` and feature branches, add feature-only commits dated older than threshold, ensure no PR exists in the PR source used by the app, then run staleness detection end-to-end and assert branch is marked stale. Include at least one non-stale control branch.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily wiring/orchestration: bootstrap + detector result + warning renderer. Concrete miss at Unit: formatter-only tests can pass while bootstrap never invokes warning path or drops branch context fields. Rejected System because behavior text is about emission when branch is already stale, not real OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire session-start module with real in-process collaborators (staleness result provider, warning emitter sink). Feed a stale current-branch state and assert one warning is emitted containing branch name, age, and suggested action text.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is a pure local mapping function with no I/O or cross-module boundary. Any higher level adds cost without exposing additional failure modes for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over representative computed branch-state inputs; assert exact label output and determinism across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo and live PR state. Concrete miss below System: integration with fake repo/PR providers can pass while real git or real PR API state produces different branch/PR linkage, causing wrong rendered status.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real repo context with real PR state source configured. Validate rendered output for two scenarios: (1) clean branch and (2) branch with extra commits and no open PR, expecting explicit STALE wording only in scenario (2).

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is cross-module state handoff between PR-status resolution and stale classifier. Concrete miss at Unit: classifier-only tests pass even if integration layer mis-maps `merged`/`open` PR statuses and still labels stale. Rejected System because behavior text does not require live API/network; deterministic in-process PR fixtures are sufficient for this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real classifier pipeline and in-process PR-state provider fixtures for `open`, `merged`, and `none`; assert only `none` can become `stale-no-pr` while `open` and `merged` remain non-stale.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is git history rewriting semantics (rebase/squash) that are hard to faithfully fake. Concrete miss below System: mocked commit graphs can treat rewritten commits as still unique, while real git ancestry after squash/rebase should show work already integrated, avoiding stale-no-PR classification.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real git repo, create feature work, then integrate via squash merge and separately via rebase+merge paths. After each history rewrite/integration, run detection and assert branch is not classified as stale-no-pr despite hash changes.
